data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the dual-channel acknowledgment mechanism — CLI preconditions-ack subcommand and interactive sprint story-closure prompt — plus sample-ack mode for classes with 4+ entries. SC5 and SC6 land together as they jointly define the ack contract consumed by 6068-cb2d.

**Why**: The sprint closure check (S3) blocks stories with unacked degradations; this story provides the user-facing mechanism to create valid ack events that unblock closure. Without this, the enforcement chain has no exit.

**Scope**:
- IN: CLI preconditions-ack subcommand accepting decision_id + required --if-skipped string (validated: non-empty after trim, >=10 characters, passes 3-word-window fuzzy substring match against precondition text using bash normalization mirroring fuzzy-match.sh; non-Latin precondition text falls back to manual-review path with explicit gate refusal message); interactive sprint story-closure prompt presenting unacked degradations and accepting ack input; ack events written per S1 frozen format (decision_ids array, if_skipped, timestamp, sampled_set, schema_version); sample-ack --class=<degradation_type> for classes with >=4 entries (deterministic: SHA256-derived selection of 3 decision_ids, selection algorithm documented, stored sampled_set in ack record, remainder absorbed only when all 3 spot-checks pass validation); ack-rationale rubric at plugins/dso/docs/contracts/ack-rationale-rubric.md documenting non-tautological substance criteria
- OUT: Writing degradation events (S2); sprint enforcement check (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLI preconditions-ack accepts a decision_id and --if-skipped string; validation rejects rationales that are empty/whitespace, under 10 characters, or fail the 3-word-window content-reference match against the precondition text; validation accepts rationales that substantively reference precondition content
  ← Satisfies: "CLI: a new dso preconditions-ack subcommand taking a decision_id and a required --if-skipped" (SC5)
- When this story is complete, the interactive sprint story-closure prompt presents all unacked degradation entries and accepts ack <decision_id> --if-skipped="..." per entry; validation identical to CLI
  ← Satisfies: "Interactive sprint prompt at story closure presents unacked degradations" (SC5)
- When this story is complete, sample-ack --class=<degradation_type> for a class with >=4 entries randomly selects exactly 3 using SHA256 of sorted decision_ids as the seed; all 3 must pass --if-skipped validation; the ack record stores the sampled_set (specific 3 decision_ids selected); remainder absorbed only when all 3 pass
  ← Satisfies: "sample-ack --class=<degradation_type> mode randomly selects 3 entries from the class" (SC6)
- When this story is complete, ack-rationale-rubric.md exists documenting the substantive rationale criteria
  ← Satisfies: "Audit precision: a periodic sample of 20 ack events shows >=80% contain a substantive...--if-skipped rationale" (SC5)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] 3-word-window fuzzy match needs comprehensive edge-case test fixture (short rationales at/below 10-char boundary, rationales that share no 3-word window, near-misses, non-Latin text triggering manual-review fallback)
- [Testing] Sample-ack determinism: use SHA256 of sorted decision_ids as the determinism source; test fixtures must specify the exact input decision_ids so expected 3-selection is pre-computable without runtime randomness; must NOT use $RANDOM or /dev/urandom without a test-override env var path
- [Reliability] Ack events use exactly the S1-frozen format; no fields may be added or removed without re-opening S1 first

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a sprint orchestrator, the dual-channel acknowledgment mechanism lets me formally acknowledge degradation fallthroughs so blocked stories can be closed with evidence the implications were reviewed
